title: Roasted Pillowy Slovakian Dish Hash
description: Hearty, pillowy dish from Slovakia, perfect for brunch with a zesty twist from lime juice.
instructions: Cut sour rye bread into 1-inch cubes and place on a baking sheet to toast for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook ground pork in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
In a separate pot, boil cabbage for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a large bowl, mix together toasted bread, cooked pork, cabbage, caraway seeds, onion, salt, and black pepper.
Crush fermented sausage and add it to the bread mixture, stirring until combined.
In a separate bowl, mix together melted butter and lime juice.
Pour the butter-lime mixture over the bread mixture, stirring until everything is well coated.
Crack in eggs and stir until the mixture is evenly moistened.
Transfer the mixture to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish and top with grated cheddar.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the eggs are set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
